description_long: This object consists of two swords, one of which is broken, both featuring straight, double-edged blades with a simple, angular geometry. The intact sword has a long, pointed blade with a flat, wide crossguard and a cylindrical handle capped with a hexagonal pommel. The broken sword mirrors the design but has a jagged, fractured blade, suggesting damage or wear. Both handles are dark in color, likely made of wood or metal, and the crossguards are flat and extend horizontally. The blades are metallic and have a matte, silvery finish, lacking any visible ornamentation or engravings. The overall design is utilitarian, with no decorative elements, and the swords appear functional rather than ceremonial.
description: A pair of straight, double-edged swords, one broken and one intact, with simple crossguards and cylindrical handles. The design is utilitarian, with matte metallic blades and no ornamentation.
description_view_0: Front view showing both the intact and broken swords side by side, highlighting the difference in blade length and the jagged break on one blade.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the thin profile of both blades and the symmetry of the crossguards and handles.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, again showing the intact and broken blades, with clear visibility of the handle shapes.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, emphasizing the slimness of the blades and the uniformity of the handles.
attribute_handle_design: cylindrical, dark, smooth, capped with hexagonal pommel
